title: Office
description: Common workplace vocabulary for professional communication

vocabulary.meaning: The latest time by which something must be completed
vocabulary.example: The project deadline is next Friday.

vocabulary.meaning: A gathering of people for discussion
vocabulary.example: We have a team meeting at 10 AM.

vocabulary.meaning: A person you work with
vocabulary.example: My colleague helped me with the report.

vocabulary.meaning: A talk given to an audience, often with slides
vocabulary.example: I'm preparing a presentation for the client.

vocabulary.meaning: Time worked beyond normal hours
vocabulary.example: I had to work overtime to finish the project.

vocabulary.meaning: An advance in position or rank at work
vocabulary.example: She received a well-deserved promotion.

vocabulary.meaning: A list of items to be discussed at a meeting
vocabulary.example: Let's review the agenda before the meeting.

vocabulary.meaning: Comments about someone's work or performance
vocabulary.example: The manager gave positive feedback on my work.

vocabulary.meaning: To generate ideas freely in a group
vocabulary.example: Let's brainstorm solutions to this problem.

vocabulary.meaning: To work jointly with others
vocabulary.example: We need to collaborate across departments.

vocabulary.meaning: To assign a task to someone else
vocabulary.example: Good managers know how to delegate tasks.

vocabulary.meaning: Key Performance Indicator — a measurable goal
vocabulary.example: We set KPIs at the beginning of each quarter.

vocabulary.meaning: Working from home or outside the office
vocabulary.example: Many companies now offer remote work options.

vocabulary.meaning: A small enclosed workspace within an open office
vocabulary.example: Each employee has their own cubicle.

vocabulary.meaning: An office layout without walls between workstations
vocabulary.example: The company uses an open plan office.

vocabulary.meaning: A system where employees do not have a fixed desk
vocabulary.translation: lam viec khong co ban rieng co dinh
vocabulary.example: Hot-desking is common in hybrid offices.

vocabulary.meaning: A large room used for senior-level meetings
vocabulary.translation: phong hop hoi dong quan tri
vocabulary.example: The board meeting is held in the boardroom.

vocabulary.meaning: A machine that produces paper copies of documents
vocabulary.example: The printer is out of paper.

vocabulary.meaning: A device that converts paper documents into digital files
vocabulary.example: Use the scanner to digitize the contract.

vocabulary.meaning: Office supplies such as pens, paper, and envelopes
vocabulary.example: Order more stationery from the supply room.

vocabulary.meaning: A large board used to write notes during meetings
vocabulary.example: Draw the diagram on the whiteboard.

vocabulary.meaning: A device that displays images on a screen
vocabulary.example: Connect the laptop to the projector.

vocabulary.meaning: A telephone call with live video
vocabulary.example: We held a video call with the overseas team.

vocabulary.meaning: A written record of what was discussed in a meeting
vocabulary.example: Please send the meeting minutes to everyone.

vocabulary.meaning: Tasks agreed upon during a meeting that must be done
vocabulary.translation: cac dau muc can thuc hien
vocabulary.example: The action items were assigned at the end of the meeting.

vocabulary.meaning: Planning and overseeing the completion of a project
vocabulary.example: Good project management ensures deadlines are met.

vocabulary.meaning: A specific output or result expected from a project
vocabulary.translation: san pham / ket qua ban giao
vocabulary.example: List all deliverables before starting the project.

vocabulary.meaning: A formal assessment of an employee's work
vocabulary.translation: danh gia nang luc lam viec
vocabulary.example: Annual performance reviews are done in December.

vocabulary.meaning: Formally leaving a job by giving notice
vocabulary.example: She handed in her resignation this morning.

vocabulary.meaning: The time between resigning and actually leaving a job
vocabulary.translation: thoi han bao truoc khi nghi viec
vocabulary.example: My notice period is two weeks.

vocabulary.meaning: A document showing how much an employee has been paid
vocabulary.example: Check your payslip at the end of each month.

vocabulary.meaning: Work arrangements where employees choose their hours
vocabulary.example: The company offers flexible hours.

vocabulary.meaning: The required standard of clothing in a workplace
vocabulary.example: Our dress code is business casual.

vocabulary.meaning: The process of integrating a new employee into the company
vocabulary.translation: dao tao hoi nhap nhan vien moi
vocabulary.example: The onboarding process takes one week.

vocabulary.meaning: A hands-on training session
vocabulary.translation: hoi thao / khoa hoc thuc hanh
vocabulary.example: We attended a leadership workshop.

vocabulary.meaning: A piece of office furniture for storing documents
vocabulary.example: Store the contract in the filing cabinet.

vocabulary.meaning: A short written message within a company
vocabulary.translation: thu noi bo / ghi chu noi bo
vocabulary.example: I sent a memo to all staff about the policy change.

vocabulary.meaning: A meeting held via telephone or online
vocabulary.translation: hop qua dien thoai / truc tuyen
vocabulary.example: We hold a weekly teleconference with the US team.

vocabulary.meaning: A bill sent to a client for payment
vocabulary.example: Send the invoice to the client by Friday.

vocabulary.meaning: A digital document for organizing data in rows and columns
vocabulary.example: Update the spreadsheet with this month's figures.

vocabulary.meaning: The amount of work someone has to do
vocabulary.example: My workload has increased this month.

vocabulary.meaning: A telephone call in which several people participate
vocabulary.example: We had a conference call with clients in New York.

vocabulary.meaning: A written or spoken account of findings or activities
vocabulary.example: Submit the monthly report by the 5th.

vocabulary.meaning: Electronic messages sent over the internet
vocabulary.example: Please email me the updated file.

vocabulary.meaning: A trial period for a new employee
vocabulary.example: She is still on probation for the first three months.

vocabulary.meaning: Paid days off work given to an employee each year
vocabulary.example: I have 15 days of annual leave remaining.

vocabulary.meaning: A fixed regular payment for employment
vocabulary.example: Her salary was increased after the promotion.

vocabulary.meaning: A temporary group assigned to tackle a specific problem
vocabulary.example: A taskforce was created to handle the crisis.

vocabulary.meaning: Time off work due to illness
vocabulary.example: She took sick leave for two days.

vocabulary.meaning: The main office of a company
vocabulary.example: The company's headquarters is in Hanoi.

vocabulary.meaning: A formal evaluation of an employee's performance
vocabulary.example: My annual appraisal is scheduled for next week.

vocabulary.meaning: A standard used for comparison or measurement
vocabulary.example: We use industry benchmarks to evaluate our performance.

vocabulary.meaning: An extra payment given to an employee as a reward
vocabulary.example: Employees receive an annual bonus based on performance.

vocabulary.meaning: A short meeting to give people important information
vocabulary.example: There is a morning briefing at 8:30 AM.

vocabulary.meaning: The amount of money available for a project or department
vocabulary.example: We need to stay within the marketing budget.

vocabulary.meaning: Physical or mental exhaustion caused by overwork
vocabulary.example: Taking breaks helps prevent burnout.

vocabulary.meaning: To send a copy of an email to additional recipients
vocabulary.example: Please cc me on all emails about the project.

vocabulary.meaning: The regular journey between home and workplace
vocabulary.example: My commute takes about 45 minutes by train.

vocabulary.meaning: A formal written agreement between employer and employee
vocabulary.example: Please review and sign the employment contract.

vocabulary.meaning: A shared office space used by freelancers and remote workers
vocabulary.example: I rented a desk at a coworking space downtown.

vocabulary.meaning: Reducing the number of employees in a company
vocabulary.example: The company announced downsizing due to budget cuts.

vocabulary.meaning: A person hired to work for a company
vocabulary.example: The company has over 500 employees worldwide.

vocabulary.meaning: A person or company that hires workers
vocabulary.example: A good employer values work-life balance.

vocabulary.meaning: A self-employed person who works for different clients
vocabulary.example: We hired a freelancer to design the website.

vocabulary.meaning: Human Resources — the department managing employees
vocabulary.example: Contact HR if you have questions about your benefits.

vocabulary.meaning: A work model combining office and remote work
vocabulary.example: Our company uses a hybrid work model — three days in office.

vocabulary.meaning: The folder where incoming emails are received
vocabulary.example: I have over 100 unread emails in my inbox.

vocabulary.meaning: A student or trainee who works to gain experience
vocabulary.example: We have two interns joining the marketing team this summer.

vocabulary.meaning: To dismiss an employee due to lack of work or budget
vocabulary.example: The company had to lay off 50 employees.

vocabulary.meaning: Time off work granted to a mother before and after childbirth
vocabulary.example: She is on maternity leave for six months.

vocabulary.meaning: To control every small detail of someone's work
vocabulary.translation: quan ly chi tiet qua muc
vocabulary.example: Good leaders trust their team and avoid micromanaging.

vocabulary.meaning: An important stage or event in the progress of a project
vocabulary.example: Completing the prototype was a major milestone.

vocabulary.meaning: Building professional relationships and contacts
vocabulary.translation: xay dung moi quan he cong viec
vocabulary.example: Networking events are great for career growth.

vocabulary.meaning: To hire an outside company to do work for your business
vocabulary.example: We outsource our IT support to a specialized firm.

vocabulary.meaning: A regular payment to a retired employee
vocabulary.example: The company offers a good pension plan.

vocabulary.meaning: The rate at which work is completed efficiently
vocabulary.example: Regular breaks can actually improve productivity.

vocabulary.meaning: A formal plan or suggestion put forward for consideration
vocabulary.example: The team submitted a proposal for the new project.

vocabulary.meaning: Happening or produced every three months
vocabulary.example: We publish quarterly sales reports.

vocabulary.meaning: To find and hire new employees
vocabulary.example: We need to recruit two more developers.

vocabulary.meaning: Money paid back to an employee for business expenses
vocabulary.example: Submit your receipts for travel reimbursement.

vocabulary.meaning: A person with an interest or concern in a project or business
vocabulary.example: We need approval from all stakeholders before proceeding.

vocabulary.meaning: A person who oversees and directs the work of others
vocabulary.translation: giam sat vien / nguoi quan ly
vocabulary.example: Report any issues to your supervisor immediately.

vocabulary.meaning: The combined effort of a group working together
vocabulary.example: Good teamwork leads to better results.

vocabulary.meaning: The rate at which employees leave and are replaced
vocabulary.example: High staff turnover can be costly for a company.

vocabulary.meaning: A healthy balance between work and personal life
vocabulary.translation: can bang cong viec va cuoc song
vocabulary.example: Work-life balance is important for employee well-being.

vocabulary.meaning: A flexible work schedule allowing employees to vary their hours
vocabulary.example: Flex time lets me pick up my children from school.

vocabulary.meaning: A system where employees share desks rather than having assigned seats
vocabulary.translation: làm việc không chỗ ngồi cố định
vocabulary.example: Hot desking reduces office space costs.

vocabulary.meaning: The beliefs and behaviors of an organization
vocabulary.example: A positive corporate culture attracts top talent.

vocabulary.meaning: A formal evaluation of an employee's work and progress
vocabulary.translation: đánh giá hiệu suất công việc
vocabulary.example: I have my annual performance review next week.

vocabulary.meaning: The process of integrating a new employee into the organization
vocabulary.translation: quy trình hội nhập nhân viên mới
vocabulary.example: The onboarding programme spans the first two weeks.

vocabulary.meaning: A document showing how much you have been paid
vocabulary.example: Check your payslip for deductions.

vocabulary.meaning: Items like pens, paper, and staplers used in an office
vocabulary.example: Order more office supplies before they run out.

vocabulary.meaning: An office with no dividing walls between workspaces
vocabulary.example: The company moved to an open-plan office to encourage collaboration.

vocabulary.meaning: A workplace model mixing remote and in-person work
vocabulary.example: A hybrid office policy requires staff in three days a week.

vocabulary.meaning: Activities designed to strengthen relationships within a team
vocabulary.example: The company does quarterly team building events.

vocabulary.meaning: Guidance given by an experienced person to help someone develop
vocabulary.example: Senior staff are encouraged to take part in mentoring.

vocabulary.meaning: Extra pay given for exceptional work performance
vocabulary.example: She earned a performance bonus for exceeding her targets.

vocabulary.meaning: Work done beyond the normal working hours
vocabulary.example: Working overtime is paid at time and a half.

vocabulary.meaning: A company-organized event away from the office for planning or bonding
vocabulary.example: We had our annual company retreat in the mountains.

vocabulary.meaning: A desk and computer area assigned to an employee
vocabulary.example: Adjust your workstation to avoid back pain.

quiz.question: What is the latest time to complete a task called?
What is a person you work with called?
What is time worked beyond normal hours?
What is a list of items discussed in a meeting?
What is physical or mental exhaustion from overwork called?
What is a formal plan put forward for consideration?
What is the journey between home and work called?
What is an important stage in a project called?
What is a student or trainee gaining work experience called?
What is paying back an employee for business expenses called?
What work model combines office and remote work?
What is the rate at which employees leave a company called?
What is a document showing how much an employee was paid?
What is the process of hiring an outside company to do work?
